name: Cheesecake Supreme
recipeInstructions:
1. Combine the flour, remaining flour, sugar, and lemon peel in a bowl. Cut in butter until crumbly. Stir in the beaten egg yolk and vanilla.
2. Pat 1/3 of the dough onto the bottom of an 8 or 9 inch springform pan (with sides removed). Bake at 400°F for 7 minutes or until golden. Cool.
3. Brush the sides of the pan with butter, attach to the bottom, and pat remaining dough onto the sides to a height of 1 3/4 inches. Set aside.
4. In a large mixing bowl, beat together the softened cream cheese, lemon peel, and vanilla. Stir in the sugar, flour, and salt mixture. Add the eggs and egg yolk, beating until combined. Stir in milk.
5. Turn into crust-lined pan. Bake at 450°F for 10 minutes. Reduce heat to 300°F and bake an additional 50-55 minutes or until the center is set and a knife inserted comes out clean. Cool 15 minutes.
6. Loosen sides of cheesecake with a spatula, cool 30 minutes, then remove sides. Cool about 2 hours longer. Chill thoroughly.
7. Top with Cherry Sauce.
Cherry Sauce:
1. In a saucepan combine sugar, cornstarch, and salt. Stir in water. Add cherries (fresh or frozen) and cook until thickened and bubbly. Cook 1-2 minutes more. Cover and chill without stirring. Alternatively, use one 21 ounce can cherry pie filling instead of the sauce.
